prompt:
Construct a ProctorOps Offline Assessment Operations Console supporting exam content governance, on-site entry validation, and compliance-grade traceability for training centers and corporate testing rooms. The application runs on Windows 11 and is designed for front-desk operators, proctors, content managers, and security administrators. Staff sign in using local username and password, with passwords at least 12 characters and locked after 5 failed attempts within 10 minutes; CAPTCHA is generated locally after the third failed attempt and clears after a 15-minute cool-down. The Qt desktop UI emphasizes keyboard-first operation (global shortcuts like Ctrl+F for advanced filters, Ctrl+K for quick command palette, and Ctrl+L to lock the console), multi-window workflows (question bank editor, check-in desk, and audit viewer open side-by-side), right-click context menus (e.g., "Map to Knowledge Point," "Mask PII," "Export for Request"), deep clipboard controls (auto-redact copied PII), and a system tray mode for "kiosk-style" check-in. Entry validation supports barcode scanner input (USB HID), manual member ID entry, or typed mobile number formatted as (###) ###-####; the console validates term-card date ranges, blocks expired/frozen accounts, and deducts punch-card sessions atomically with duplicate-submission prevention (same member + same session cannot be checked in twice within 30 seconds). When offline data is collected at multiple desks, deferred sync is handled by exporting/importing signed LAN share packages or USB files, with conflict prompts for double-deductions and a required supervisor override for corrections.

Built with Qt for the cross-platform-ready desktop interface and SQLite for local persistence, the data model includes Questions, KnowledgePoints (chapter/tree), many-to-many mappings, tags, and attributes such as difficulty (1–5) and discrimination (0.00–1.00), plus a query builder supporting combined filters (e.g., "Chapter 3 AND difficulty ≥4 AND tag='Safety'"). A local crawl orchestration module is repurposed as an internal batch ingestion scheduler for importing question files and check-in rosters from the file system: manual and scheduled triggers, job priorities, dependency chains (validate → import → index), concurrency limits (default 2 workers), exponential retry backoff (5s/30s/2m, max 5 tries), failure isolation, and resumable checkpoints so interrupted imports continue after restart. Security safeguards include AES-256 encryption for sensitive fields (mobile number, member identifiers), masked display by default (show last 4 digits), TLS is not used because no internet endpoints are allowed, and all access is local with role-based permissions and step-up verification (re-enter password within 2 minutes) for sensitive actions like permission changes, mass deletions, or overriding freezes. Tamper-evident audit logs record login, check-ins, content edits, and administrative actions with hash-chained entries, retention set to 3 years by default, and offline workflows for data export/deletion requests aligned to GDPR and China MLPS concepts. The app must cold-start in under 3 seconds on a typical office PC, run continuously for 7 days without memory growth beyond 200 MB, and provide automatic state recovery after crashes, plus offline update/rollback via importing a signed installer package (.msi) from disk.
